GlobalPolicy.module-level-focus: Fast deterministic checks for vvoc schema v3 layout, role parsing, `vv-role:*` resolution, managed agent prompt scaffolding, managed agent registration, role preset application, Guardian/Memory built-in role wiring, main-session system guidance injection, memory CRUD, and secrets redaction.
GlobalPolicy.wave-level-focus: Typecheck, lint, format-check, and test the merged CLI + plugin surface after each coordinated role-system change.
GlobalPolicy.phase-level-focus: Buildable package output plus stable command behavior for installation, role management, role resolution, preset switching, primary-session system guidance, Guardian review, explicit memory workflows, and secrets redaction.
GlobalPolicy.redaction: Never log secrets, credentials, raw tokens, or high-risk payloads outside the existing Guardian truncation policy.

VF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.NAME: PluginInstall
VF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.scenario: User runs `vvoc init` or `vvoc install` to bootstrap the package into a fresh global or project config root.
VF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.expected-outcome: The pinned vvoc package specifier is present in the resolved OpenCode `plugin` array, the managed enhancer agent and managed subagents are registered in OpenCode config with `vv-role:*` model references, managed prompts exist in the scope-correct agents directory, and the canonical `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/vvoc.json` file is fully seeded with built-in roles and built-in role presets.

required-signals.trace-sequence: resolvePaths -> ensurePackageInstalled -> syncManagedAgentRegistrations -> installManagedAgentPrompts -> installVvocConfig -> status reports configured role-based setup

VF-CONFIG-SYNC.NAME: ManagedConfigSync
VF-CONFIG-SYNC.scenario: User syncs managed vvoc config after changing package versions or moving to another device.
VF-CONFIG-SYNC.expected-outcome: Canonical vvoc schema v3 and managed prompt files are rewritten canonically, the managed enhancer/subagent registration stays present in OpenCode config with `vv-role:*` references, managed `vv-*` presets are refreshed to vvoc defaults, current explicit role-based values survive the vvoc.json rewrite where applicable, and pre-role vvoc schemas fail loudly instead of being migrated.

required-signals.trace-sequence: inspect canonical config -> parse current role values -> render canonical JSON -> syncManagedAgentRegistrations

VF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.NAME: RoleManagement
VF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.scenario: User runs `vvoc role list`, `vvoc role set`, or `vvoc role unset`.
VF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.expected-outcome: Built-in roles are always shown, custom roles can be created or removed, concrete assignments are normalized as `provider/model[:variant]`, and built-in roles cannot be removed accidentally.

required-signals.trace-sequence: read canonical vvoc.json -> validate role id -> normalize assignment -> write role map or render inventory

VF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.NAME: RoleResolution
VF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.scenario: OpenCode loads config that contains `model: vv-role:<role-id>` in supported root, agent, or command fields.
VF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.expected-outcome: Supported fields resolve to concrete model selections at startup, variant-bearing assignments split into native `model` plus `variant` fields where supported, and unknown roles fail loudly with a field-specific error.

required-signals.trace-sequence: load canonical role map -> inspect model-bearing config fields -> resolve `vv-role:*` values -> mutate OpenCode config object or throw explicit error

VF-PRESET-SWITCH.NAME: PresetSwitch
VF-PRESET-SWITCH.scenario: User applies a named preset such as `vvoc preset vv-openai` or `vvoc preset vv-zai`.
VF-PRESET-SWITCH.expected-outcome: Only the roles declared in the selected preset change, unlisted roles remain untouched, the canonical vvoc role map is updated, and OpenCode config stays role-reference based rather than being rewritten per preset.

required-signals.trace-sequence: read canonical vvoc.json -> resolve preset -> write listed role assignments -> print per-role summary

VF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.NAME: GuardianReview
VF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.scenario: OpenCode asks for a permission decision while Guardian is active.
VF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.expected-outcome: Guardian resolves its runtime model from the built-in `fast` role, low-risk actions may be auto-approved, and higher-risk actions fall back to manual approval.

VF-MEMORY-ACCESS.NAME: MemoryAccess
VF-MEMORY-ACCESS.scenario: User stores and retrieves explicit memory across session, branch, project, and shared scopes.
VF-MEMORY-ACCESS.expected-outcome: Session, branch, and project entries stay project-local, shared entries are visible across projects, and stored memory is never injected into prompts automatically, while the agent still receives a short system reminder to use memory tools proactively.

required-signals.trace-sequence: system reminder appended -> loadMemoryRuntimeConfig -> putMemory/listMemories/searchMemories -> correct local/shared file layout

VF-MEMORY-REVIEW.NAME: MemoryReview
VF-MEMORY-REVIEW.scenario: User invokes `@memory-reviewer` to audit existing memory.
VF-MEMORY-REVIEW.expected-outcome: The reviewer resolves its runtime model from the built-in `fast` role, can inspect memory with read-only tools, and returns a report without changing entries.

required-signals.trace-sequence: plugin config registers memory-reviewer -> reviewer role resolves -> reviewer uses memory_list/get/search only

VF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.NAME: SystemContextInjection
VF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.scenario: User starts a primary chat session with vvoc installed.
VF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.expected-outcome: The main-session user message carries reusable vvoc system guidance such as proactive explore usage, while known subagents do not inherit that guidance.

required-signals.trace-sequence: chat.message -> resolved primary agent -> append message.system guidance -> skip known subagents

VF-SECRETS-REDACTION.NAME: SecretsRedaction
VF-SECRETS-REDACTION.scenario: LLM request is sent with secrets in message history, tool inputs, or tool outputs.
VF-SECRETS-REDACTION.expected-outcome: Secrets are replaced with `__VVOC_SECRET_*` placeholders before LLM request, restored after LLM response, and restored before tool execution.

VM-ROLE-PARSING.scenario: Parse and normalize built-in and custom role ids, `vv-role:*` references, and concrete `provider/model[:variant]` assignments.
VM-ROLE-PARSING.trajectory-evidence: exception-path assertions for malformed ids, malformed assignments, and unknown roles
VM-ROLE-PARSING.telemetry: none required beyond explicit thrown-error surfaces

VM-CONFIG-SEED.scenario: Install or init seeds schema v3, built-in roles, managed `vv-*` role presets, and role-reference OpenCode defaults plus managed agents.
VM-CONFIG-SEED.primary-evidence: temp-home integration checks plus exact config assertions
VM-CONFIG-SEED.trajectory-evidence: OpenCode config diff assertions proving `vv-role:*` writes instead of literal provider/model writes
VM-CONFIG-SEED.telemetry: installation inspection output plus stable config snapshots

VM-CONFIG-SYNC.scenario: Sync preserves user-owned custom roles while refreshing managed role presets and rejecting unsupported pre-role schemas.
VM-CONFIG-SYNC.trajectory-evidence: trace assertions on parse -> refresh -> rewrite order
VM-CONFIG-SYNC.telemetry: stable rewrite summaries and doctor/status inspection snapshots

VM-ROLE-CLI.scenario: Role list, set, and unset mutate only canonical vvoc.json and protect built-in roles.
VM-ROLE-CLI.trajectory-evidence: failure assertions for builtin unsets and invalid role ids
VM-ROLE-CLI.telemetry: CLI summaries only; no provider-bound logs required

VM-PRESET-ROLES.scenario: Preset application rewrites only listed role assignments and leaves OpenCode config untouched because it is already role-reference based.
VM-PRESET-ROLES.primary-evidence: deterministic vvoc.json assertions plus untouched OpenCode config assertions
VM-PRESET-ROLES.trajectory-evidence: per-role summary ordering and no-op coverage for unlisted roles

VM-RUNTIME-RESOLUTION.scenario: Plugin config hooks resolve role references in supported root, agent, and command fields, split variants where supported, and fail loudly when resolution is impossible.
VM-RUNTIME-RESOLUTION.trajectory-evidence: trace assertions on resolution count, field-path error reporting, and forbidden fallback behavior
VM-RUNTIME-RESOLUTION.telemetry: structured config-hook markers for map load, per-field resolution, and unresolved roles

VM-GUARDIAN-ROLE.scenario: Guardian loads non-model runtime policy from vvoc.json, resolves its runtime model from the built-in `fast` role, and logs bounded permission-review evidence.
VM-GUARDIAN-ROLE.primary-evidence: plugin-config assertions plus bounded review-path tests
VM-GUARDIAN-ROLE.trajectory-evidence: required block-marker order for runtime-config load and permission review
VM-GUARDIAN-ROLE.telemetry: structured guardian runtime-config and review markers

VM-MEMORY-ROLE.scenario: Memory reviewer resolves its runtime model from the built-in `fast` role while memory storage keeps its explicit-only semantics.
VM-MEMORY-ROLE.primary-evidence: plugin-config assertions plus memory-tool availability assertions
VM-MEMORY-ROLE.trajectory-evidence: reviewer-registration trace assertions and disabled-memory failure assertions
VM-MEMORY-ROLE.telemetry: structured memory plugin initialization markers with role-resolution context

TelemetryRequirements.TR-1: `ModelRolesPlugin` must emit structured markers anchored to `BLOCK_RESOLVE_CONFIG_ROLE_REFERENCES` for role-map load, successful per-field resolution, and unresolved-role failure paths without logging raw secrets or provider credentials.
TelemetryRequirements.TR-2: Guardian runtime config loading and permission review must emit stable markers anchored to `BLOCK_LOAD_GUARDIAN_RUNTIME_CONFIG` and `BLOCK_REVIEW_PERMISSION_REQUEST` so failures can be triaged without replaying full conversations.
TelemetryRequirements.TR-3: Memory initialization and reviewer registration must emit stable markers anchored to `BLOCK_INITIALIZE_MEMORY_PLUGIN` and `BLOCK_REVIEWER_AGENT_CONFIGURATION`, while memory payload content stays redacted or omitted.

scenarios.scenario-1: Built-in role ids `default`, `smart`, `fast`, and `vision` are exposed deterministically.
scenarios.scenario-2: `vv-role:*` references parse and round-trip cleanly for valid role ids.
scenarios.scenario-4: Unknown roles, malformed role ids, and malformed model selections fail with field-specific errors.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Role resolution must not allow role-to-role chaining in the first implementation wave.

V-M-MODEL-ROLES.wave-follow-up: Add table-driven fixtures for provider alias assignments and custom role ids.

scenarios.scenario-1: Fresh install config renders with schema v3, seeded built-in roles, and a pinned vvoc package specifier in the plugin array.
scenarios.scenario-2: Existing JSONC comments survive plugin insertion and managed OpenCode role-reference rewrites.
scenarios.scenario-3: Global path resolution separates `opencode/` from the canonical `vvoc/vvoc.json` path while project scope still uses `./.vvoc/agents/` for prompts.
scenarios.scenario-4: Managed enhancer/subagent registrations point OpenCode at scope-correct prompt references and use `vv-role:*` model values instead of hardcoded provider/model strings.
scenarios.scenario-5: Canonical vvoc.json is fully seeded, section writes preserve unrelated sections, and managed `vv-*` role presets are refreshed during canonical rewrites.
scenarios.scenario-6: Install and sync write root OpenCode `model = vv-role:default`, `small_model = vv-role:fast`, built-in OpenCode agent role references, and managed agent role references instead of concrete provider/model values.
scenarios.scenario-7: Strict reads and sync fail on unsupported pre-role vvoc schema versions instead of migrating them.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Sync must keep the canonical role map in vvoc.json and keep OpenCode config role-reference based instead of storing concrete target overrides there.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Install and sync must not materialize literal provider/model defaults into managed OpenCode model-bearing fields, except inside lower-level provider alias patch objects.

V-M-CLI-CONFIG.wave-follow-up: Add temp-home smoke tests for `status` and `doctor` so unresolved role references and built-in role assignments are surfaced deterministically.
V-M-CLI-CONFIG.phase-follow-up: Add snapshot coverage for rendered OpenCode config that includes all managed role-reference fields in one fixture.

scenarios.scenario-1: CLI compiles and registers all planned top-level subcommands including `role`, `preset`, `patch-provider`, and `config validate`.
scenarios.scenario-2: `guardian config --print` still emits valid guardian section JSON content without any model ownership.
scenarios.scenario-3: `vvoc role list` reports built-in roles first and custom roles after them.
scenarios.scenario-4: `vvoc status` and `vvoc doctor` report role assignments and unresolved role problems without depending on the removed `vvoc agent` surface.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Command handlers should delegate file mutation to helper utilities instead of duplicating filesystem logic.

V-M-CLI-COMMANDS.wave-follow-up: Verify `vvoc install`, `vvoc sync`, `vvoc role list`, and `vvoc doctor` against a temp config home.

scenarios.scenario-1: The plugin resolves `vv-role:*` references in root `model` and `small_model` fields.
scenarios.scenario-2: The plugin resolves `vv-role:*` references in `agent.*.model` and `command.*.model` fields.
scenarios.scenario-3: Variant-bearing assignments are written as native `model` plus `variant` fields for agent targets that support variants.
scenarios.scenario-4: Literal provider/model values that do not use the `vv-role:*` syntax remain untouched.
scenarios.scenario-5: Unknown role references throw explicit errors that identify the config field path.
scenarios.scenario-6: Root `model` and `small_model` fail clearly when a role assignment resolves to a variant-bearing concrete selection that cannot be represented there safely.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The plugin must not rewrite literal provider/model values that do not use the `vv-role:*` syntax.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Agent targets that support variants must split `provider/model:variant` into native `model` plus `variant` fields rather than leaving a colon suffix in `model`.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: Role resolution must not silently fall back to built-in provider defaults when a referenced role is missing.

V-M-PLUGIN-MODEL-ROLES.wave-follow-up: Add fixtures that mix root defaults, built-in agents, and custom commands in a single config object.
V-M-PLUGIN-MODEL-ROLES.phase-follow-up: Add a smoke harness that loads a synthetic OpenCode config object with both built-in and custom role references through the plugin hook.

scenarios.scenario-1: Guardian plugin config registers guardian as a hidden subagent with an explicit two-step limit.
scenarios.scenario-2: Guardian resolves its runtime model from the built-in `fast` role without depending on `ModelRolesPlugin` hook ordering.
scenarios.scenario-3: Guardian initialization fails loudly when the built-in `fast` role cannot be resolved to a valid concrete model selection.
scenarios.scenario-4: Review failures fall back to manual approval instead of silently allowing.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Auto-allow only occurs when Guardian returns a risk score below the configured threshold.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The built-in `fast` role must be resolved before Guardian agent registration is exposed to OpenCode runtime hooks.

V-M-PLUGIN-GUARDIAN.wave-follow-up: Add isolated behavioral tests around assessment parsing and decision thresholds.
V-M-PLUGIN-GUARDIAN.phase-follow-up: Add a temp-config harness that verifies Guardian role resolution and env override precedence if model env escapes remain supported.

scenarios.scenario-1: Session, branch, and project entries stay project-local while shared entries are visible across projects.
scenarios.scenario-2: Search returns the best lexical match in the requested scope.
scenarios.scenario-3: Update and delete preserve store consistency and clean empty directories.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Memory stays explicit-only and no store operation injects entries into prompts automatically.

V-M-PLUGIN-MEMORY-STORE.wave-follow-up: Watch performance if the number of JSON entries grows significantly.

scenarios.scenario-1: MemoryPlugin registers all six memory tools.
scenarios.scenario-2: Plugin config registers the `memory-reviewer` subagent without an explicit `steps` limit.
scenarios.scenario-3: `memory-reviewer` resolves its runtime model from the built-in `fast` role and exposes only read-only memory tools.
scenarios.scenario-4: Plugin appends a short system instruction encouraging proactive memory tool usage without injecting stored entries.
scenarios.scenario-5: Disabled memory config blocks tool use instead of pretending memory exists.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The reviewer agent must not have write/delete memory tools enabled.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The system reminder must not include stored memory entries or imply that memory is already loaded.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: The plugin must not continue reading `reviewerModel` or `reviewerVariant` from canonical memory config once role-based selection lands.

V-M-PLUGIN-MEMORY.wave-follow-up: Consider adding a smoke test around custom tool execution in a live OpenCode session.
V-M-PLUGIN-MEMORY.phase-follow-up: Add temp-config coverage for built-in `fast` role resolution and for disabled-memory mode after role-based reviewer wiring lands.

scenarios.scenario-1: Primary agents such as `build` and `enhancer` receive the injected system guidance exactly once.
scenarios.scenario-2: Known subagents such as `explore`, `implementer`, and `memory-reviewer` do not receive the injected guidance.
scenarios.scenario-3: Custom config-defined subagents stay excluded after the plugin observes config mode metadata.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The plugin must mutate `message.system` through `chat.message`, not through the global system transform hook, so agent filtering remains possible.

V-M-PLUGIN-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.wave-follow-up: Consider making the injected system contexts configurable once multiple reusable guidance blocks exist.

scenarios.scenario-1: Plugin exports compile against current OpenCode plugin and SDK types.
scenarios.scenario-2: Placeholders are stable within a session (same secret maps to same placeholder).
scenarios.scenario-3: Builtin patterns detect the documented token and identifier classes.
scenarios.scenario-4: Disabled config makes plugin a no-op.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: HMAC hash is irreversible without the session secret.

V-M-PLUGIN-SECRETS-REDACTION.wave-follow-up: Add live session smoke coverage once a safe harness exists for observing provider-bound payloads.

scenarios.scenario-1: `role list` shows the seeded built-in roles in deterministic order.
scenarios.scenario-2: `role set my-role provider/model:variant` stores a normalized concrete assignment.
scenarios.scenario-3: `role set smart provider/model:variant` updates a built-in role without changing unrelated built-ins or custom roles.
scenarios.scenario-4: `role unset default` fails with a clear built-in-role protection error.
scenarios.scenario-5: Invalid role ids or malformed concrete model selections fail before any file write occurs.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The role command must mutate only canonical vvoc.json and must not write OpenCode config directly.

V-M-CLI-ROLE.wave-follow-up: Add smoke tests for mixed built-in and custom role inventories.

scenarios.scenario-1: A fully seeded canonical vvoc schema v3, including the managed `vv-*` role preset map, reports OK.
scenarios.scenario-2: Invalid JSON reports parse error with line/column.
scenarios.scenario-3: Missing required built-in role ids, invalid role assignment strings, or invalid field types report the schema path and error.
scenarios.scenario-4: Presets with malformed role assignments fail semantic validation with the offending preset and role path.
scenarios.scenario-5: Pre-role vvoc schema versions report an unsupported-version failure instead of being accepted leniently.

V-M-CLI-CONFIG-VALIDATE.wave-follow-up: Run against real config files in the repository.

scenarios.scenario-1: `preset list` shows the default `vv-openai`, `vv-zai`, and `vv-minimax` presets.
scenarios.scenario-2: `preset show vv-openai` renders the stored preset object from canonical vvoc.json, including a smart-role assignment that can point at `openai/vv-gpt-5.4-xhigh`.
scenarios.scenario-3: Applying a partial preset only changes the roles listed in that preset and leaves unlisted roles untouched.
scenarios.scenario-4: Preset application updates canonical vvoc role assignments and does not rewrite OpenCode config directly.
scenarios.scenario-5: Built-in managed presets refresh back to vvoc defaults during sync even if the user edited a managed preset name in-place.
scenarios.scenario-6: Unknown preset names fail with a clear error that includes the available preset names.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Preset application must reuse canonical vvoc write helpers instead of introducing a parallel mutation path.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Preset application must never reset roles that are not explicitly listed in the selected preset.

V-M-CLI-PRESET.wave-follow-up: Consider dynamic shell completion for custom user-defined preset names if the CLI grows a config-discovery completion layer.

scenarios.scenario-1: Lists all plugins when OpenCode config has a populated plugin array.
scenarios.scenario-2: Shows `ModelRolesPlugin` when the package exports are wired correctly.

V-M-CLI-PLUGIN-LIST.wave-follow-up: Run against a temp config with known plugin entries.

scenarios.scenario-1: `vvoc patch-provider stepfun-ai` writes `provider.stepfun.options.baseURL = https://api.stepfun.ai/v1` into the global OpenCode config.
scenarios.scenario-2: `vvoc patch-provider zai` writes the `provider.zai-coding-plan.models.glm-4.5-airx` patch into the global OpenCode config without clobbering sibling model entries.
scenarios.scenario-3: `vvoc patch-provider openai` writes only `provider.openai.models.vv-gpt-5.4-xhigh` and does not overwrite root `model` or `small_model`, because those fields are role-reference based.
scenarios.scenario-4: Unsupported preset names fail with a clear list of supported presets.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The command must fail rather than overwrite non-object `provider` config shapes.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The command must not rewrite root role-reference OpenCode model fields while installing provider alias-model helpers.

V-M-CLI-PATCH-PROVIDER.wave-follow-up: Add more presets only when a concrete endpoint mismatch needs a one-shot patch.

scenarios.scenario-1: Bash completion script contains the current vvoc top-level commands plus nested completions for `config`, `plugin`, `patch-provider`, `preset`, and `role` including built-in role ids.
scenarios.scenario-2: Zsh completion script registers `vvoc` via `#compdef` and `compdef` markers so the installed file works when sourced or loaded from completion paths.
scenarios.scenario-3: Fish completion script exposes nested completions for `role set|unset <role-id>` using built-in role IDs.
scenarios.scenario-4: Completion output removes the obsolete `agent` command surface and its target-id completions.

V-M-CLI-COMPLETION.wave-follow-up: Validate generated scripts by sourcing/eval in test environment.

scenarios.scenario-1: Interactive prompts are shown when TTY is available.
scenarios.scenario-2: Ctrl+C aborts cleanly without partial file writes.
scenarios.scenario-3: Generated canonical config is valid schema v3 JSON with seeded built-in roles and managed `vv-*` role presets.
scenarios.scenario-4: Non-interactive init seeds OpenCode defaults and managed agents with `vv-role:*` references.
scenarios.scenario-5: Status and doctor output immediately after init report the built-in role assignments that were seeded.

V-M-CLI-INIT.wave-follow-up: Test with a temp directory and verify file contents.

scenarios.scenario-1: Reports `already latest` when npm returns same version.
scenarios.scenario-2: When npm returns a newer version, the command runs exactly `bun add -g @osovv/vv-opencode@<latest>` and then runs `vvoc sync` without project scope arguments.
scenarios.scenario-3: Registry or network failure is caught and reported gracefully.

V-M-CLI-UPGRADE.wave-follow-up: Mock npm registry responses and subprocess execution so upgrade coverage never performs real global installs.

Gate-Phase-1.goal: CLI foundation is buildable and the helper layer has deterministic checks.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Config merge and installation inspection are covered by module-local tests.

Gate-Phase-2.goal: Guardian is packaged from the repo and exported without behavior drift.

required-evidence.evidence-1: `src/plugins/guardian/index.ts` is present and package root exports `GuardianPlugin`.

Gate-Phase-3.goal: Explicit memory is packaged from the repo and stored under the vvoc XDG data root with a report-only reviewer agent.

required-evidence.evidence-1: `src/plugins/memory/index.ts` and `src/plugins/memory-store.ts` are present and package root exports `MemoryPlugin`.

Gate-Phase-4.goal: CLI enhancement commands are implemented, buildable, and integrated into the CLI.

required-evidence.evidence-1: `vvoc plugin list`, `vvoc config validate`, `vvoc completion`, `vvoc init`, and `vvoc upgrade` all register as CLI commands.

Gate-Phase-6.goal: The role-based model system replaces target overrides end-to-end and shared workflows resolve semantic model references deterministically.

commands.command-4: bun test src/lib/model-roles.test.ts src/plugins/model-roles.test.ts src/commands/role.test.ts src/commands/preset.test.ts src/commands/config-validate.test.ts src/plugins/guardian.test.ts src/plugins/memory.test.ts src/lib/opencode.test.ts src/commands/completion.test.ts

required-evidence.evidence-2: OpenCode defaults and managed agents use `vv-role:*` references and runtime startup resolves them to concrete models.
required-evidence.evidence-3: Pre-role vvoc schema versions are rejected rather than migrated.
required-evidence.evidence-4: Structured markers for role resolution, Guardian runtime config, and memory reviewer registration are present or otherwise replaced with equally stable structured telemetry before autonomous execution is enabled.

packet-template.expected-fields: scenario, contract-ref, expected-evidence, observed-evidence, first-divergent-block, suggested-next-action
